summary: No ship: R-10 and the RED verify-record are sound, and no new critical issue was found. R-11 remains false-GREENable, however. Simpler alternative: none materially simpler than the mandatory lane design; strengthen its proof. Open question: none.

findings.title: Blocker: R-11's structural approval seal still permits false GREENs
findings.body: The plan claims `propose-pr` can never arm and the workflow gate enforces verbatim planner forwarding. The committed gate only proves that an `.action` assignment exists and each `--action` argument is textually `$action`; it accepts `action=$(...jq -r .action); action=bump; ... --action "$action"`. The executor tests also cover `propose-pr` only for create and skip, so an implementation that arms it only during adopt/rebuild can pass. Either path makes an `autoDeploy:false` PR auto-merge while the suite is GREEN, reproducing R-11's approval bypass.
findings.recommendation: Add a hermetic call-site witness that feeds both planner lanes through the production loop and asserts the executor's actual argv, including a post-read `action=bump` negative candidate. Add `propose-pr` witnesses for adopt and rebuild, then re-pin red.sha and regenerate the machine-owned RED record.

next_steps: Close both R-11 false-GREEN paths in the committed witnesses.
Re-pin red.sha and regenerate the RED verify-record without manually running the regression suite.
